body.p1: A full node downloads, verifies, and relays HobbyHash Coin blocks and transactions. Nodes matter because they independently validate the chain instead of trusting a third-party server. Inbound nodes also help other peers find and sync the network.
body.p10: Checksums are available only when published on the downloads page. Do not invent or trust checksums from unofficial sources.
body.p2: Outbound-only nodes can still sync, but inbound nodes help more because other peers can connect to them.
body.p3: The requested AL9 Linux package path is:
body.p4: Coming soon: that package file is not currently present in the public downloads folder. Do not use mirror links or invented downloads.
body.p5: Use this as a starting point and replace the RPC credentials before running a node.
body.p6: Use getblockchaininfo to check height and sync status. Use getnetworkinfo to check peer count and network details. Stop the daemon safely with hobbyhash-cli stop.
body.p7: View the node debug log in the configured data directory when troubleshooting startup, peers, or sync. The exact data directory path depends on how the node is installed and configured.
body.p8: For resync basics, stop the node cleanly first, keep wallet backups safe, and only remove chain/index data when you understand what each directory contains. Never delete wallet files while troubleshooting chain sync.
body.p9: Bootstrap snapshot: Coming soon. No official bootstrap snapshot is confirmed in the current site.
hero.lead: Install and run a HOBC full node safely on Linux.
hero.title: Linux Node
list.firewall.1: Forward 18761/tcp only if you want inbound mainnet peers.
list.firewall.3: For simple Linux firewalls, allow inbound TCP 18761 only.
list.firewall.4: RPC should remain reachable only from the same machine: 127.0.0.1.
list.install.1: Download the official package from hobbyhashcoin.com/downloads when available.
list.install.3: Extract the package into a directory you control.
list.install.4: Create the config file with private RPC settings.
list.install.6: Watch logs until the node begins syncing.
list.recommended.1: Use a stable Linux server, home server, or always-on machine.
list.recommended.2: Use reliable storage with enough free disk space for chain growth.
list.recommended.4: Forward only mainnet P2P port 18761 if you want inbound peers.
list.recommended.5: Keep RPC bound to localhost and never expose RPC to the internet.
meta.description: Install and run the HOBC Linux node safely.
meta.title: Linux Node
